description: Trigger and behavior cases for strict red-green-refactor: confirmed failure reasons, minimal implementation, refactor-under-green, and honest command transcripts.

cases.prompt: Build the discount calculator TDD-style: 10% off orders over $100, 20% over $500, never stacking with promo codes.

assertions: States the contract as concrete cases before any code, and writes the first failing test before any production code.
Runs the test and quotes the failure message proving it failed for the intended reason, not an import/setup error.
Implements the minimal change per iteration and refactors only while green, re-running the suite after refactoring.
Reports exact commands and real output for every red and green.

cases.prompt: Add a test-first guard so empty cart checkout returns a validation error instead of a 500.

assertions: If the new test passes immediately, treats it as a red flag: investigates whether the behavior already exists, the assertion is tautological, or the wrong path is exercised.
Does not proceed to 'implementation' of behavior that already exists; reports the finding instead.

cases.prompt: Play around with the new charting library and see if it can even render our dataset shapes — nothing decided yet.

assertions: No settled contract exists; a time-boxed spike is appropriate, and TDD against a guessed contract would harden the guess.

cases.prompt: TDD the new webhook signature verification into the api module.

assertions: If the module's existing suite is already failing before work starts, reports the red baseline and stops for a decision instead of building on top of it or fixing unrelated tests unprompted.
Does not misattribute pre-existing failures to the new work or vice versa.
